# Cost Forecast — 2026-07-10

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-07-07 → 2026-07-09 (ACA post-grant-reset actuals, n=3) + 2026-07-03 → 2026-07-09 (non-ACA trailing 7d)
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

## Headline

| Horizon          | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta    | Confidence |
|------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|---------:|------------|
| Rest of month (10–31 Jul) | £12.76     | £24.78         | −£12.02  | Medium-High |
| Next 30 days (10 Jul–9 Aug) | £15.30   | £36.44         | −£21.14  | Medium     |
| Next quarter (10 Jul–8 Oct) | £47.42   | £133.00        | −£85.58  | Low        |

**TL;DR:** The July free-grant window closed on **6 Jul** (ACA billing resumed 7 Jul — three days earlier than last week's ±2-day estimate), giving three clean post-reset days of evidence. The real post-grant ACA rate came in at **£0.4485/day**, ~30% above last week's two-day estimate of £0.345/day — driven by an unusually high shipping week (near-point endpoint, anonymous search, per-IP rate limiting, digest rebrand: ~19 merges in 4 days, several triggering redeploys). Blended steady state is now **~£0.49/day (~£14.71/month)**, up from last week's £11.60/month but still a small, healthy number. Azure's forecast remains overstated on every horizon (1.9×–2.8×) and — new finding this week — is **internally inconsistent depending on query window length**: the same calendar date (17 Jul) forecasts £1.72, £2.13, or £7.44 depending on whether the query spans 30, 21, or 90 days. The 90-day query reproduces the exact £7.44/day "phantom plateau" figure flagged in the 2026-06-27/28/29 and 2026-07-06 reports — it hasn't gone away, it's just invisible at shorter query windows.

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **Azure's forecast is not stable across query-window length — a new, concrete finding.** Querying the forecast API for the same calendar date (2026-07-17) returns three different numbers depending on how far out the query horizon extends: **£1.72** (31-day query), **£2.13** (21-day query), **£7.44** (90-day query). The 90-day query reproduces, digit for digit, the same £7.43–7.44/day "phantom plateau" this skill's reports have flagged every week since 2026-06-27 — it re-extrapolates the mid-June Cosmos ramp for 13–17 Jul and again on 21 Jul (rows: 12 Jul £5.41, 13–17 Jul £7.44 flat, 18 Jul £5.40, 19 Jul £1.59, 20 Jul £4.27, 21 Jul £7.44). Recomputing the rest-of-month total from the 90-day query's July rows gives **£70.64** — nearly identical to last week's headline Azure figure of £72.04 for the same window, which strongly suggests last week's report (or the portal tile) was sourced from a longer-window query that carries this artifact, while this week's shorter, horizon-matched queries mostly avoid it. Cosmos (`cosmos-town-crier-shared`) has been destroyed since 2026-06-27 (commits `21e8fda3`, `26b1604a`) with zero cost every day since (confirmed down to £0.0001 on 27 Jun, £0 every day after) — no resource in the subscription can produce £7.44/day today.
- **ACA grant-reset timing, confirmed and refined.** Last week's report predicted paid ACA billing would resume "~10 Jul, ±2 days" based on June's pattern (grant lasted through 9 Jun). It actually resumed **7 Jul** — 3 days early, at the edge of the stated error band. This means July's free-grant window (6 days: 1–6 Jul) was shorter than June's (9 days: 1–9 Jun), consistent with rising baseline consumption (more anonymous-traffic endpoints, higher deploy frequency). Azure's linear model has no way to know the grant reset already happened and re-happens monthly; it instead spreads a blended historical average across the whole horizon.
- **June's migration and cutover churn continues to decay out of the lookback window but hasn't fully cleared it.** The Cosmos→Postgres cutover (26–28 Jun), the .NET→Go dual-stack period, and Cosmos's final ramp (up to £1.56/day, 25 Jun) still sit inside any 60–90-day lookback Azure's forecast model uses. As those days age further out of the window over the coming weeks, expect Azure's forecast to keep converging toward the bottom-up number — as it partially has done this week (rest-of-month delta narrowed from a 6.5× overstatement on 06 Jul to 1.9× today, when comparing like-for-like short-window queries).
- **Where Azure is accidentally right:** Postgres and Storage. Its linear model projects £0 for both because actuals are £0 — correct by coincidence, not because it knows about the free-tier offer or the low-traffic blob usage.

## Recommendations

No new cost-saving opportunities crossed the £2/month bar this cycle — this was a "confirm and refresh" week, updating the three open beads from prior reports with fresh evidence rather than filing new ones.

- **Lower the subscription monthly budget from £50 to £20.**
  **Estimated saving:** £0/month (guard-rail value, zero effort)
  **Bead:** `tc-0qnj` (notes updated this run — still open, not yet actioned; live budget confirmed still £50 via `az consumption budget list`)
  **Rationale:** Steady state has risen slightly to ~£14.71/month (from ~£11.60 last week) as ACA's post-grant rate climbed with this week's shipping velocity. The existing £50 budget still only fires its 100% alert on a ~3.4× anomaly. £20 remains the right target: the 50% alert (£10) trips near normal month-end, the 100% alert flags genuine runaway spend.

- **Confirm the Postgres free-tier offer's terms and expiry date.**
  **Estimated saving:** n/a — filed as a question (impact **+£13.50/month** when the offer lapses)
  **Bead:** `tc-jiad9` (notes updated this run — reconfirmed £0.00 across all 10 days of July to date)
  **Rationale:** Still riding entirely on zero-rated free-tier meters. No new information on the lapse date this cycle; still needed for FY budgeting.

- **Dev Container App revision accumulation (`ca-town-crier-api-go-dev`).**
  **Estimated saving:** £0/month direct (dev scales to zero, so idle revisions cost no compute) — this is an operational-risk item, not a spend item
  **Bead:** `tc-fxau` (already open since 2026-06-17, notes updated this run — not a new finding)
  **Rationale:** 22 active revisions observed today (Fri 10 Jul), consistent with the known pattern: the weekly `dev-container-app-cleanup.yml` job (last run 6 Jul, succeeded) resets to 1 every Monday, and ~4.8 revisions/day accumulate afterwards because the inline per-deploy deactivation in `deploy-container-app/action.yml` silently no-ops for dev's `Multiple`/`latestRevision` traffic config (root cause identified 2026-06-19, still unfixed). At this rate, ~36–37 revisions are expected by Monday's reset — comfortably short of the 100-revision platform ceiling, so risk remains bounded as previously assessed. No status change.

**Watch items (no bead — below threshold or not yet actionable):**

- **ACA post-grant rate is rising (£0.345 → £0.4485/day) and the free-grant window is shrinking (9 free days in June → 6 in July).** Both point the same direction: real product usage and shipping cadence are climbing, which is expected for a product with its first paying customer, not a waste signal. Worth re-checking next report whether the trend continues into August's grant-reset date.
- **`log-town-crier-shared` ingestion dropped ~5× this week** (£0.0049 → £0.0007/day) with no corresponding infra commit found. Below the threshold to investigate — noting it in case it recurs or reverses.
- **ACR storage growth slowed** (16.6 MB in 4 days vs 330 MB/week last report) — still tiny (429.6 MB of 10 GB, 4.2%), no action needed.

**Checked and cleared:** prod `minReplicas: 1` unchanged (cold-start fix, paying customer); dev API scale-to-zero unchanged; prod has exactly 1 active revision (Single mode, working as designed); no drift between Pulumi (dev=22, prod=26, shared=38 resources) and Azure resource census (39 total, resource-type mix unchanged from last report — no Cosmos, no new resource types); the four infra commits since the 06 Jul report (`4090ca13`, `d6ec071d`, `ba6ca74e`, `8841da04`) are drift-check silencing + a Dependabot bump, none cost-relevant; ACR has no revision-triggered overage; Service Bus still Basic; no untagged billable resources (the 7 untagged items are free platform-created artifacts: managed certs, Smart Detection action group, Service Health alert).

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals window:** 2026-05-11 → 2026-07-10 (60 days), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` and by `ServiceName`, via Cost Management REST API (`api-version=2023-11-01`). 2026-07-10 is a partial day and excluded from all run-rates.
- **Split baseline:** ACA resources (`ca-town-crier-api-go-*`, `job-tc-*`) use 7–9 Jul (3 clean post-grant-reset days, n=3 — flagged Medium confidence, not Low, since this is 50% more evidence than last week's n=2). Non-ACA resources use a 7-day trailing window (3–9 Jul) since no change-point applies to them.
- **Grant-reset model:** confirmed directly this cycle — ACA actuals show exactly £0.00–0.13 (non-ACA-only) for 1–6 Jul, then a step up to £0.33–0.70/day from 7 Jul. Modelled forward as: July has 0 remaining free days (already exhausted before "today"); August is assumed to repeat July's pattern (6 free days, 1–6 Aug) for the Next-30-days and Next-quarter horizons — this is an extrapolation from a single data point (July) and is the dominant source of the "Low" confidence rating on the quarter horizon. September and beyond assume the same 6-day pattern with no further refinement.
- **Azure forecast queries:** three separate `forecast` API calls, one per horizon, each spanning exactly today→horizon-end (21, 31, and 91 days respectively) — matching the skill's per-horizon convention. A fourth diagnostic pull (the 91-day query) was used to demonstrate the query-window-dependency finding in "Why Azure's forecast is wrong"; it is not used for the rest-of-month or 30-day headline numbers.
- **Postgres free tier:** reconfirmed at meter level via the resource-level actuals — `psql-town-crier-shared` billed exactly £0.00 every day from 1–10 Jul (10 consecutive days), same B1MS Burstable / 32 GB Premium_LRS config as last report. Lapse risk tracked in `tc-jiad9`.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** all three stacks re-exported (`dev`=22, `prod`=26, `shared`=38 resources). Resource-type census against the live `az resource list` snapshot is unchanged from the 06 Jul report's full reconciliation (same types, same counts of Container Apps/Jobs/managed identities/storage accounts) — no new drift found. Did not re-run a full URN-by-URN match this cycle since no infra commits landed that would move resources between stacks; the lighter census check is the stated shortcut.
- **Exclusions:** all Cosmos-era history; the 26–28 Jun cutover churn; 2026-07-10 (partial day).
